description:
compile_fn currently tracks variable representations using two parallel sets:

  let mut raw_f64_vars: HashSet<u32> = HashSet::new();
  let mut raw_int_vars: HashSet<u32> = HashSet::new();

These are passed as separate arguments to every helper that needs to know a
variable's representation. There is no enforced invariant preventing a variable
from appearing in both sets simultaneously.

The free function:
  fn var_repr(v: u32, raw_int_vars: &HashSet<u32>, raw_f64_vars: &HashSet<u32>) -> ArgRepr

encodes the query logic and is threaded through ~10 call sites.

Replace with:

  enum VarRepr { Tagged, RawInt, RawF64 }
  let mut var_reprs: HashMap<u32, VarRepr> = HashMap::new();

Insertions become:
  var_reprs.insert(v, VarRepr::RawInt);   // was raw_int_vars.insert(v)
  var_reprs.insert(v, VarRepr::RawF64);   // was raw_f64_vars.insert(v)

Queries at call sites:
  var_reprs.get(&v).copied().unwrap_or(VarRepr::Tagged)
  // was: var_repr(v, &raw_int_vars, &raw_f64_vars)

Remove:
  - raw_f64_vars declaration and all insertions
  - raw_int_vars declaration and all insertions
  - var_repr() free function definition
  - Every parameter site that passed both sets to a helper

Update every helper function signature that accepted raw_int_vars and
raw_f64_vars to instead accept var_reprs: &HashMap<u32, VarRepr> (or a
shared reference to the same map).

Proven: all existing tests pass. The VarRepr enum enforces mutual exclusion
at the type level.

## LANDING THE WORK
  Land this ticket before starting the next one:
  - Commit only the changes for this ticket, referencing fz-cg1.4
  - Close the ticket (`bw close fz-v9l.4`); It will tell you if work is newly unblocked
title: fz-cg1.4 replace dual HashSet var tracking with VarRepr enum map; remove var_repr()
